feature: Inventory and dependency graph: experiment catalog / cost
user_story: As an operator, I can understand and act on experiment catalog cost posture without leaving Mayyam.
ship_slice: Ship as one vertical slice for experiment catalog: data contract, collector or tool adapter, backend API, deterministic evaluator, UI surface, tests, documentation, and operator runbook.
implementation_detail: Discover experiment catalog, relationships, ownership, labels, versions, and runtime dependencies for the cost pillar.
backend_scope: Add collector, normalized model, repository methods, and freshness tracking for experiment catalog.
frontend_scope: Add list/detail views, dependency graph, filters, and export for experiment catalog.
api_contract: Expose authenticated API endpoints for Chaos and Reliability Engineering experiment catalog inventory with pagination, freshness metadata, audit IDs, error codes, and export support.
data_model: experiment-catalog_cost_inventory evidence, score, finding, recommendation, owner, suppression, audit event
deterministic_scope: Implement deterministic checks for experiment catalog cost that can run without an LLM, emit reason codes, and preserve raw evidence.
agentic_scope: Allow agentic investigation only through registered tools for experiment catalog; read-only diagnostics are default, mutations require explicit approval, and every tool call is replayable.
interaction_model: Support inspect, compare, diagnose, dry-run, approve, execute, rollback-note, audit, and export workflows for experiment catalog.
cost_opportunity: Identify savings opportunities for experiment catalog, quantify estimated monthly impact, confidence, effort, risk, and verification steps.
risk_controls: Require RBAC, scoped credentials, rate limits, stale-data detection, blast-radius summary, suppression policy, and rollback or recovery notes for experiment catalog.
telemetry_contract: Emit collection duration, freshness age, success/failure counts, rule evaluation count, AI/tool-call count when applicable, and user action audit metrics for experiment catalog.
test_plan: Add unit tests for evaluator logic, fixture tests for collected evidence, API contract tests, UI render/action tests, and one failure-path test for stale or missing experiment catalog data.
rollout_guardrail: Gate behind a feature flag, support read-only mode first, document required permissions, and add rollback or disable instructions before enabling experiment catalog inventory broadly.
docs_runbook: Document prerequisites, permissions, setup, known limits, common failure modes, triage commands, remediation approval path, and verification checks for experiment catalog.
acceptance_criteria: Given experiment catalog data exists, when the cost inventory workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.
dependency: experiment catalog connector credentials, normalized resource identity, sync-run freshness, RBAC, audit logging

ship_slice: Ship as one vertical slice for availability zone outage drill: data contract, collector or tool adapter, backend API, deterministic evaluator, UI surface, tests, documentation, and operator runbook.
implementation_detail: Support read, validate, mutate, rollback, and audit workflows for availability zone outage drill with resilience guardrails.
backend_scope: Add validated commands, dry-run support, RBAC checks, and audit records for availability zone outage drill.
frontend_scope: Add action menu, confirmations, diff previews, and result status for availability zone outage drill.
api_contract: Expose authenticated API endpoints for Chaos and Reliability Engineering availability zone outage drill operations with pagination, freshness metadata, audit IDs, error codes, and export support.
data_model: availability-zone-outage-drill_resilience_operations evidence, score, finding, recommendation, owner, suppression, audit event
deterministic_scope: Implement deterministic checks for availability zone outage drill resilience that can run without an LLM, emit reason codes, and preserve raw evidence.
agentic_scope: Allow agentic investigation only through registered tools for availability zone outage drill; read-only diagnostics are default, mutations require explicit approval, and every tool call is replayable.
interaction_model: Support inspect, compare, diagnose, dry-run, approve, execute, rollback-note, audit, and export workflows for availability zone outage drill.
cost_opportunity: Record any cost side effect for availability zone outage drill recommendations so non-cost changes do not create hidden spend regressions.
risk_controls: Require RBAC, scoped credentials, rate limits, stale-data detection, blast-radius summary, suppression policy, and rollback or recovery notes for availability zone outage drill.
telemetry_contract: Emit collection duration, freshness age, success/failure counts, rule evaluation count, AI/tool-call count when applicable, and user action audit metrics for availability zone outage drill.
test_plan: Add unit tests for evaluator logic, fixture tests for collected evidence, API contract tests, UI render/action tests, and one failure-path test for stale or missing availability zone outage drill data.
rollout_guardrail: Gate behind a feature flag, support read-only mode first, document required permissions, and add rollback or disable instructions before enabling availability zone outage drill operations broadly.
docs_runbook: Document prerequisites, permissions, setup, known limits, common failure modes, triage commands, remediation approval path, and verification checks for availability zone outage drill.
acceptance_criteria: Given availability zone outage drill data exists, when the resilience operations workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.
dependency: availability zone outage drill connector credentials, normalized resource identity, sync-run freshness, RBAC, audit logging

ship_slice: Ship as one vertical slice for experiment schedule: data contract, collector or tool adapter, backend API, deterministic evaluator, UI surface, tests, documentation, and operator runbook.
implementation_detail: Detect experiment schedule symptoms, thresholds, anomalies, and correlated events that affect performance.
backend_scope: Add deterministic evaluators, metrics ingestion, and explainable findings for experiment schedule.
frontend_scope: Add health badges, charts, timelines, and affected-object drilldowns for experiment schedule.
api_contract: Expose authenticated API endpoints for Chaos and Reliability Engineering experiment schedule health with pagination, freshness metadata, audit IDs, error codes, and export support.
data_model: experiment-schedule_performance_health evidence, score, finding, recommendation, owner, suppression, audit event
deterministic_scope: Implement deterministic checks for experiment schedule performance that can run without an LLM, emit reason codes, and preserve raw evidence.
agentic_scope: Allow agentic investigation only through registered tools for experiment schedule; read-only diagnostics are default, mutations require explicit approval, and every tool call is replayable.
interaction_model: Support inspect, compare, diagnose, dry-run, approve, execute, rollback-note, audit, and export workflows for experiment schedule.
cost_opportunity: Record any cost side effect for experiment schedule recommendations so non-cost changes do not create hidden spend regressions.
risk_controls: Require RBAC, scoped credentials, rate limits, stale-data detection, blast-radius summary, suppression policy, and rollback or recovery notes for experiment schedule.
telemetry_contract: Emit collection duration, freshness age, success/failure counts, rule evaluation count, AI/tool-call count when applicable, and user action audit metrics for experiment schedule.
test_plan: Add unit tests for evaluator logic, fixture tests for collected evidence, API contract tests, UI render/action tests, and one failure-path test for stale or missing experiment schedule data.
rollout_guardrail: Gate behind a feature flag, support read-only mode first, document required permissions, and add rollback or disable instructions before enabling experiment schedule health broadly.
docs_runbook: Document prerequisites, permissions, setup, known limits, common failure modes, triage commands, remediation approval path, and verification checks for experiment schedule.
acceptance_criteria: Given experiment schedule data exists, when the performance health workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.
dependency: experiment schedule connector credentials, normalized resource identity, sync-run freshness, RBAC, audit logging

ship_slice: Ship as one vertical slice for experiment result analysis: data contract, collector or tool adapter, backend API, deterministic evaluator, UI surface, tests, documentation, and operator runbook.
implementation_detail: Summarize experiment result analysis evidence, likely causes, confidence, uncertainty, and next diagnostic commands for cost issues.
backend_scope: Add context builder, prompt template, evidence citations, and LLM safety constraints for experiment result analysis.
frontend_scope: Add triage panel, follow-up prompts, saved notes, and feedback loop for experiment result analysis.
api_contract: Expose authenticated API endpoints for Chaos and Reliability Engineering experiment result analysis ai-triage with pagination, freshness metadata, audit IDs, error codes, and export support.
data_model: experiment-result-analysis_cost_ai-triage evidence, score, finding, recommendation, owner, suppression, audit event
deterministic_scope: Implement deterministic checks for experiment result analysis cost that can run without an LLM, emit reason codes, and preserve raw evidence.
agentic_scope: Allow agentic investigation only through registered tools for experiment result analysis; read-only diagnostics are default, mutations require explicit approval, and every tool call is replayable.
interaction_model: Support inspect, compare, diagnose, dry-run, approve, execute, rollback-note, audit, and export workflows for experiment result analysis.
cost_opportunity: Identify savings opportunities for experiment result analysis, quantify estimated monthly impact, confidence, effort, risk, and verification steps.
risk_controls: Require RBAC, scoped credentials, rate limits, stale-data detection, blast-radius summary, suppression policy, and rollback or recovery notes for experiment result analysis.
telemetry_contract: Emit collection duration, freshness age, success/failure counts, rule evaluation count, AI/tool-call count when applicable, and user action audit metrics for experiment result analysis.
test_plan: Add unit tests for evaluator logic, fixture tests for collected evidence, API contract tests, UI render/action tests, and one failure-path test for stale or missing experiment result analysis data.
rollout_guardrail: Gate behind a feature flag, support read-only mode first, document required permissions, and add rollback or disable instructions before enabling experiment result analysis ai-triage broadly.
docs_runbook: Document prerequisites, permissions, setup, known limits, common failure modes, triage commands, remediation approval path, and verification checks for experiment result analysis.
acceptance_criteria: Given experiment result analysis data exists, when the cost ai-triage workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.
dependency: experiment result analysis connector credentials, normalized resource identity, sync-run freshness, RBAC, audit logging
